summary:
For evaluating delayed urinary output in neonates, an ultrasound is preferred as it avoids potential harms from other imaging techniques while providing necessary diagnostic clarity regarding possible obstructions within their urinary tract system efficiently yet safely. This approach aligns well considering current medical guidelines prioritizing minimally invasive procedures especially when dealing with vulnerable populations such as infants who cannot communicate effectively about symptoms they may be facing during critical developmental stages early after birth.
The optimal initial investigation would indeed leverage ultrasonography owing primarily because this technique offers detailed anatomical visualization crucially needed here alongside being entirely
